item.title: An Andy Burnham-led government would face zero-hours dilemma
item.description:
Speculation grows over how an Andy Burnham-led government may approach employment law and labour market regulation. The post An Andy Burnham-led government would face zero-hours dilemma appeared first on Personnel Today . It landed in the last few hours.
— via Personnel Today

item.title: 15 must-read business books by black authors that will help you thrive professionally
item.description:
If you’re serious about your career, then chances are you’re constantly on the hunt for resources to help you prosper at work. The most successful leaders are readers, and it’s a no-brainer that books offer the most useful guides on how to reach your professional goals. No matter what stage you’re at in your career, the list of the top 15 business books by… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: How AI Is Changing the Way UK Recruiters Source Candidates in 2026
item.description:
For most of the last decade, sourcing in the UK has looked broadly the same. A recruiter opens LinkedIn, builds... The post How AI Is Changing the Way UK Recruiters Source Candidates in 2026 first appeared on HR News .
— via HR News UK

item.title: Christmas parties in June? World Cup defenders on LinkedIn?
item.description:
HR has taken to organising Christmas parties in June; at last a good use for LinkedIn - recruiting a World Cup defender The post Christmas parties in June? World Cup defenders on LinkedIn? appeared first on Personnel Today .
— via Personnel Today

item.title: What’s missing in the Neet skills puzzle?
item.description:
With youth unemployment making headlines, how can we solve the conundrum where young people can’t find work and employers can’t find talent? The post What’s missing in the Neet skills puzzle? appeared first on Personnel Today .
— via Personnel Today

item.title: How to take a vacation as a solopreneur
item.description:
When I worked a corporate job, I had unlimited PTO . And I easily took 6+ weeks of time off every year. I have kids and followed their school schedule, taking time off for spring break and Christmas (for example). Now, I’m a solopreneur . When I started my own business, I was certain that I wouldn’t want to change how much time I take off each year. But to… It landed in the last few hours.
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Why salary sacrifice has moved from HR ‘nice-to-have’ to boardroom priority
item.description:
Matthew Walters, Head of Consultancy, Ayvens UK April 2026 marked the completion of the first full financial year under the new employer National... The post Why salary sacrifice has moved from HR ‘nice-to-have’ to boardroom priority first appeared on HR News .
— via HR News UK

item.title: World Cup or not, high performers get these 3 things wrong about pressure
item.description:
If you have watched a goalkeeper face a penalty kick, you know you are watching 0.3 seconds of pure attentional reckoning. The goalkeeper who dives before the ball is struck did not panic or fail to lean on their training. Their attention was misplaced. The stress of the situation pulled their attentional system toward irrelevant information, triggering a…
— via Fast Company Work Life

item.title: Front-line training gaps are thwarting workforce readiness, survey finds
item.description:
Training initiatives must deliver on practical skills employers deem critical and speak to employee ambitions for advancement and leadership, according to a Chegg report.
— via HR Dive

item.title: Why EAPs Must Evolve as AI Reshapes Employee Wellbeing
item.description:
https://www.hrdconnect.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/06/Red-Text-28.png As AI becomes increasingly embedded in the workplace, organisations are grappling with new wellbeing challenges ranging from job insecurity and workforce anxiety to the growing use of chatbots for emotional support. Karl Bennett, Chair of EAPA UK and founder of The Wellbeing Consortium,…
— via HRD Connect

item.title: 9 Individual Development Plan Examples With Ready-To-Use Templates
item.description:
Professional development supports both the company and the individual. Organizations benefit when employees grow their skills, and employees value workplaces that invest in their long-term development. According to TalentLMS, 73% of employees say stronger learning and development opportunities would make them stay longer at their company. Individual…
— via AIHR

item.title: 10 Best Learning and Development Courses To Consider
item.description:
The best learning and development courses help you build the skills your next HR role actually needs. With so many L&D courses available, the right choice depends less on ratings and more on fit (your role, goals, experience, and the type of proof you need). This article explains what makes an L&D course worth your… The post 10 Best Learning and Development… It landed in the last few hours.
— via AIHR

item.title: Europe’s skilling dividend: Turning talent into growth
item.description:
Europe’s workforce is a source of strength, but limited mobility holds people back. Organizations that make it easier for workers to move across roles, regions, and sectors can help accelerate growth.
— via McKinsey

item.title: The Algorithmic-Human Manager: AI, Apps, and Workers in the Indian Gig Economy
item.description:
This paper examines the impact of artificial intelligence and digital technologies on the blue-collar gig economy in India, focusing on algorithmic management. This paper examines the impact of artificial intelligence and digital technologies on the blue collar gig economy in India, focusing on algorithmic management he use of automated systems to allocate,…
— via arXiv
